question_text: Which of the following best describes what a fuse does in a household circuit?

options.label: A
options.text: It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes dangerously high

options.label: B
options.text: It stores electrical energy for later use

options.label: C
options.text: It slows down the flow of electricity to make it safer

options.label: D
options.text: It converts electrical energy into heat energy

explanation: A fuse contains a thin wire that melts if too much current flows through it, breaking the circuit and preventing damage or fire. It's a safety device that stops excessive current, not a storage device or a way to reduce current speed.
